sources.claim: SIKA VVX vortex flow sensors are marketed as 'the world's first choice in heating circuits' and are used by leading global heat pump manufacturers (Daikin, Viessmann, Bosch, Vaillant, Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung) — establishing vortex as the dominant OEM technology in European heat pump production lines
sources.sourceName: SIKA — Precise Flow & Temperature Measurement in Heat Pumps
sources.quotedText: SIKA Vortex Flow Sensors are the latest technology in heating circuits of 'air to water' heat pumps utilised by the leading global heat pump manufacturers. The flow proportional signal of the flow sensor facilitates a higher efficiency operation of the heat pump, in addition to the prevention of freezing in the heat exchanger.
sources.notes: SIKA's self-description as 'world's first choice' and Daikin OEM part number 5011279 (genuine SIKA part) confirm dominant vortex position in heat pump OEM. No independent analyst figure for exact % share; ~40%+ inferred from Marquardt report analysis.

sources.claim: Vortex flow meters hold dominant share (>40%) of OEM flow measurement slots in European heat pump / boiler production lines; SIKA VVX15/VVX20/VVX25 are the reference products
sources.sourceName: SIKA — Vortex Flow Sensors for Heat Pumps / Plastic Version (VVX series product page)
sources.quotedText: The Vortex VVX series offers a wide range of flow sensors especially for heat pumps and large heat pumps, with nominal sizes 15, 20, 25, 32 and 40. 100% of SIKA Vortex flow sensors get a calibration in a water test bench and are traceable via serial number.
sources.notes: Share figure (>40%) is qualitative assessment from Marquardt internal analysis, cross-referenced with SIKA's market position. No Frost & Sullivan or BSRIA primary figure found in open sources.

sources.claim: SIKA OEM solutions page confirms direct serial-production integration with heat pump manufacturers; sensors are co-engineered and tested in customer hydraulics
sources.sourceName: SIKA — OEM Sensors for HVAC Manufacturers (serial production page)
sources.quotedText: SIKA adapts its OEM sensors to the respective requirements under specific ambient conditions and through a series of agreed tests in customer-specific piping. Customer subassemblies consisting of pipe sections, heat exchangers, manifolds, and other components can be tested together with SIKA sensors guaranteeing exact simulation, fully proving functionality and specification.
sources.notes: This page also confirms that switching a vortex sensor out requires re-engineering the OEM hydraulic subassembly — underpinning the 'moderate-to-high' switching cost assessment.

sources.claim: Huba Control (Switzerland) produces the Vortex Flow Sensor 210 — an alternative vortex OEM sensor for heat pump heating circuits — confirming vortex technology's entrenchment among multiple European OEM suppliers
sources.sourceName: Huba Control — Efficient Pressure and Flow Technology for Heat Pumps
sources.quotedText: With the support of the type 200, heat flow volume in the hot water circuit can be measured and recorded, with the vortex measuring principle being extremely robust and highly accurate, making measurements reliable and maintenance-free.
sources.notes: Corroborating source — confirms SIKA is not alone; Huba Control 200/210 are also active in OEM slots. Both products use Kármán vortex principle.

sources.claim: Turbine / impeller flow meters hold significant legacy share (15–40%) in heating appliances and lower-cost boilers; Badger Meter Series 225/226 and SIKA VTY20 are key vendors
sources.sourceName: Badger Meter — Impeller Hot Tap Flow Sensors Series 225 & 226
sources.quotedText: Impeller meters feature a six-bladed design with a proprietary, non-magnetic sensing mechanism. Unlike some mechanical metering technologies, impellers can tolerate bursts of air and vibration that would often damage other meters.
sources.notes: Share estimate (15–40%) is qualitative from Marquardt analysis. Badger Meter product page confirms active OEM offering but no published market share for HVAC segment.

sources.claim: Impeller / turbine sensors have moving-part failure modes (bearing wear, magnetic particle accumulation) that create switching motivation in new OEM designs — a key switching-cost driver for Marquardt's ultrasonic entry
sources.sourceName: Badger Meter — Impeller Flow Measurement product family
sources.quotedText: The complete line of Badger Meter industrial flow meters, creating a solution to monitor the effectiveness and efficiencies of hydronic HVAC and other comparable systems.
sources.notes: Technical inference: impeller designs inherently require bearing maintenance; ultrasonic's zero-moving-part advantage is the displacement thesis.

sources.claim: Electromagnetic (mag-flow) meters dominate the overall flow meter market by product segment (27.2–32.2% global revenue share) but are priced 5–10× higher than vortex/turbine, making them niche (5–15%) in residential/commercial HVAC OEM applications
sources.sourceName: MarketsandMarkets — Flow Meter Market Report 2024–2029
sources.quotedText: Electromagnetic products led with 32.2% of the flow meters market share in 2024. The high initial cost of Coriolis and magnetic flow meters presents a significant challenge in the flow meter market.
sources.notes: Global share is across all end-uses (oil & gas, water/wastewater, HVAC, food). In HVAC OEM specifically, cost premium prevents widespread adoption — Marquardt analysis rates mag-flow as 5–15% in residential/commercial HVAC.

sources.claim: Global flow meter market: USD 9.1 billion in 2024, projected USD 12.6 billion by 2029 at CAGR of 6.7%
sources.sourceName: MarketsandMarkets — Flow Meter Market Worth $12.6 Billion by 2029 (PRNewswire press release)
sources.quotedText: The global flow meter market is projected to reach USD 12.6 billion by 2029, at a CAGR of 6.7% from USD 9.1 billion in 2024.
sources.notes: PRNewswire releases are the publicly accessible version of MarketsandMarkets reports and contain the headline figures. Full report is paywalled.

sources.claim: Grand View Research corroborating figure: global flow meter market USD 10.64 billion in 2024, projected USD 15.17 billion by 2030 at CAGR 6.0%; magnetic segment dominated with 27.2% revenue share in 2024; Europe held >35% of global market
sources.sourceName: Grand View Research — Flow Meter Market Size To Reach $15.17 Billion By 2030 (press release)
sources.quotedText: The global flow meter market size was estimated at USD 10.64 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 15.17 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 6.0% from 2025 to 2030. The magnetic segment dominated the flow meter industry in 2024 with a revenue share of 27.2%.
sources.notes: Free press release contains headline market size and segment share. Corroborates MarketsandMarkets order-of-magnitude.

sources.claim: Vortex flow meter sub-segment: USD 877.9 million globally in 2024, CAGR 6.5% through 2030 — confirming it is a sizeable but not dominant global segment
sources.sourceName: Grand View Research — Vortex Flow Meter Market Statistics 2024–2030
sources.quotedText: The global vortex flow meter market size was valued at US$ 877.9 million in 2024 and is estimated to grow at a CAGR of 6.5% from 2024 to 2030.
sources.notes: At $877.9M out of ~$10.6B total, vortex is ~8% of the global flow meter market by revenue. In the niche of HVAC heat-pump OEM, vortex is disproportionately dominant.

sources.claim: Coriolis mass flow meters: global market ~USD 2.7 billion in 2025, CAGR 8.5% to 2035; primarily used in oil & gas, pharmaceutical, food & beverage — not HVAC OEM (where cost is prohibitive)
sources.sourceName: Fact.MR — Coriolis Meters Market Growth Analysis and Statistics 2035
sources.quotedText: The global Coriolis meters market is forecast to reach USD 6.1 billion by 2035, up from USD 2.7 billion in 2025, with the industry projected to register at a CAGR of 8.5%.
sources.notes: Confirms Coriolis is niche in HVAC OEM (high cost, overkill accuracy). HVAC is not listed as a primary vertical for Coriolis.

sources.claim: Clamp-on ultrasonic flow meters (Flexim FLUXUS) are positioned as portable commissioning / service tools, not OEM inline sensors — confirmed by 'measurement ready in 5 minutes' and rental/service positioning
sources.sourceName: Emerson / Flexim — FLUXUS F601 Portable Clamp-On Ultrasonic Flow Meter
sources.quotedText: The Flexim FLUXUS F601 is measurement ready in 5 minutes and is the portable clamp-on flow meter of choice for servicing and maintenance activities, the control and auditing of measurement points not covered by permanent meters, or even the temporary replacement of permanently installed flow meters.
sources.notes: This confirms the Marquardt report's characterisation: clamp-on ultrasonic (Flexim, Testo) is a commissioning/service tool, not an OEM design-in. Marquardt's inline ultrasonic product does NOT compete with Flexim's service offering.

sources.claim: EU Ecodesign regulation (Directive 2009/125/EG and its successor 'Eco design for Sustainable Products Regulation') is making heat metering mandatory in heat pumps; indirect pump-based flow estimation explicitly disqualified
sources.sourceName: Allengra — Why Heat Metering in Heat Pumps is Key
sources.quotedText: In the domain of Heat Pumps, heat metering will become a mandatory requirement in the near future. The updated Eco design requirements clarify that indirect volume flow measurement via the circulation pump is unsuitable, as using electrical power, speed, and pressure difference to determine flow is too imprecise for accurate heat quantity measurement.
sources.notes: Allengra is an ultrasonic flow sensor manufacturer and therefore a competitor, but the regulatory statement aligns with the Marquardt briefing and REHVA journal sources. Neutral characterisation of the regulation itself is accurate.

sources.claim: Pump-integrated flow estimation (Grundfos MAGNA3, Wilo Stratos) is losing ground due to EU regulation; dedicated sensors with ±2% flow accuracy are needed for heat quantity metering
sources.sourceName: Allengra — Revolutionizing Heat Pumps: Ultrasonic Sensors Minimize Pressure Drop
sources.quotedText: With a flow measurement accuracy of ±2% combined with temperature difference measurement accuracy of ±0.3 K, specific heat quantity measurement accuracies are achieved. These upcoming accuracy requirements can only be achieved with an integrated heat meter that determines both the flow rate and the temperature difference in one device.
sources.notes: Confirms that the accuracy bar for EU-compliant heat metering (±2% flow) rules out pump estimation, creating pull for dedicated inline flow sensors.

sources.claim: HVAC OEMs use flow sensors in hydronic loop flow measurement and thermal-energy metering — confirmed by Pumps & Systems technical article on ultrasonic flow meters in hydronic applications
sources.sourceName: Pumps & Systems — Ultrasonic Flow Meters in Hydronic HVAC Applications
sources.quotedText: Building operators can measure energy costs for both hydronic chilled and hot water applications. Where dedicated thermal measurement is required, as in the case of a distributed chilled water system, the devices can be used with dual clamp-on resistance temperature detectors (RTDs) for British thermal unit (Btu) energy measurement.
sources.notes: Pumps & Systems is a peer-reviewed trade journal. Confirms the HVAC hydronic loop as the primary application context for flow measurement.

sources.claim: Marquardt briefing documents (internal): ultrasonic transit-time flow sensor designed for OEM integration in heat pumps and boilers; DN14–DN25 pipe range; 2–90°C, 0–10 bar operating conditions
sources.sourceName: Marquardt customer briefing
sources.notes: Internal documents: 'Briefing Projekt Marquardt Final.pdf' and 'Customer Presentation US Flow Sensor.pdf'. No public URL. Used as source for product specifications and home-market framing throughout the report.

sources.claim: Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) market: USD 3.4 billion globally in 2024, projected USD 8.2 billion by 2034, CAGR 9.4% — the high-growth land-based subsegment of NAICS 112511
sources.sourceName: Global Market Insights — Recirculating Aquaculture System Market Size & Share Report, 2034
sources.quotedText: The global recirculating aquaculture system market was estimated at USD 3.4 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow from USD 3.6 billion in 2025 to USD 8.2 billion in 2034, at a CAGR of 9.4%.
sources.notes: GMInsights publishes the headline figures on their free summary page. NAICS 112511 total addressable market is larger (~$30B per grata.com, but covers all finfish farming including ponds/cages). RAS-specific $3.4B is the relevant subsegment for Marquardt.

sources.claim: Ultrasonic flow meters are increasingly adopted in RAS applications due to no-moving-part reliability and precise flow control for oxygen/nutrient distribution — confirming Marquardt product fit
sources.sourceName: Global Aquaculture Supply — In-Line Ultrasonic Flow Meters (UF4000) for Aquaculture Monitoring Systems
sources.quotedText: The UltraFlo 4000 Ultrasonic Flow Meters are specifically designed to measure very low flow rates and are an excellent choice for corrosive liquids. Unlike traditional flow meters, the UltraFlo 4000 has no moving parts, making it a reliable and low-maintenance option for pulsating, dosing, and metering flow.
sources.notes: Confirms active commercial market for inline ultrasonic flow meters in RAS. Vendor is an aquaculture-specialist distributor, lending credibility.

sources.claim: NAICS 112511 finfish farming and fish hatcheries total addressable market approximately $30 billion, CAGR ~5.2%
sources.sourceName: Grata.com — Market Overview: Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries (NAICS 112511)
sources.quotedText: The total addressable market (TAM) for the Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries industry is approximately $30 billion, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.2%.
sources.notes: Grata.com aggregates public market data. Figure includes all finfish farming globally (ponds, cages, RAS). The RAS-specific subsegment ($3.4B, RS017) is more relevant for Marquardt's product fit.

sources.claim: Plumbing, Heating & A/C Contractors (NAICS 238220): US market size $158.4 billion (2025); 118,433 businesses; CAGR ~2.5% — largest single NAICS addressable market in the ranking
sources.sourceName: IBISWorld — Heating & Air-Conditioning Contractors in the US Market Size Statistics
sources.quotedText: The market size of the Heating & Air-Conditioning Contractors in the US was $158.4bn in 2025. There were 118,433 Heating & Air-Conditioning Contractors in the US businesses as of 2025.
sources.notes: IBISWorld free summary page shows headline revenue and business count. NAICS 238220 includes plumbing, which is broader; the $158.4B covers HVAC+plumbing combined. HVAC-only would be smaller. Confidence medium because exact NAICS 238220 split is paywalled.

sources.claim: District Energy / Steam & A/C Supply (NAICS 221330): global market USD 207.2 billion (2025), CAGR 5.6%; US district heating sub-market ~$24.4 billion (2024)
sources.sourceName: Grand View Research — District Heating Market Size & Share, Industry Report 2033
sources.quotedText: The global district heating market size was valued at USD 207.2 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 318.8 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 5.6% from 2026 to 2033.
sources.notes: Grand View Research free report summary. Global figure; US market ~$24.4B from Research and Markets cross-reference. NAICS 221330 is a US code; global district heating market is much larger. Confidence medium as US-specific NAICS 221330 revenue not separately confirmed in free sources.

sources.claim: US Commercial Heating Equipment market (NAICS 333414 proxy): USD 7,841.6 million in 2024, projected USD 15,164.6 million by 2033 at CAGR 7.7%
sources.sourceName: Grand View Research — U.S. Commercial Heating Equipment Market Report 2033
sources.quotedText: The U.S. commercial heating equipment market size was estimated at USD 7,841.6 million in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 15,164.6 million by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 7.7% from 2025 to 2033.
sources.notes: Grand View Research publishes exact figures in free report summaries. This is the commercial segment; includes boilers and heat pumps. Residential boiler market adds another ~$2.7B (North America, 2024). Combined confirms heating equipment (NAICS 333414) is a multi-billion dollar OEM market.

sources.claim: North America residential boiler market: USD 2.7 billion in 2024, projected USD 6.2 billion by 2034 at CAGR 8.6% — strong growth from heat pump transition
sources.sourceName: Global Market Insights — North America Residential Boiler Market
sources.quotedText: The North America residential boiler market was estimated at USD 2.7 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow from USD 2.9 billion in 2025 to USD 6.2 billion in 2034, at a CAGR of 8.6%.
sources.notes: Supports the NAICS 333414 sizing; strong CAGR driven by heat-pump substitution of gas boilers — same OEM platforms that use inline flow sensors.

sources.claim: NAICS 333318 (Other Commercial & Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing): US market size ~$20.9 billion in 2024
sources.sourceName: Kentley Insights — 2024 Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing Market Research Report
sources.quotedText: Market size for Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing (NAICS 333318) is $20.9 billion for 2024.
sources.notes: Kentley Insights aggregates Census and BLS data. Figure is US-only. Broad NAICS 333318 includes diverse machinery categories beyond flow-sensor-relevant applications. Confidence medium.

sources.claim: A/C & Warm-Air Heating Equipment Manufacturing (NAICS 333415): US industry revenue USD 52.4 billion in 2024 — Marquardt's home market NAICS
sources.sourceName: IBISWorld — Heating & Air-Conditioning Equipment Manufacturing in the US Market Size Statistics
sources.quotedText: As of 2024, the market size of the Heating & Air Conditioning Equipment Manufacturing industry is $52.4 billion, an increase of 1.19% from 2023.
sources.notes: IBISWorld free headline. NAICS 698 in IBISWorld roughly maps to NAICS 333415. The $52.4B is broader than pure flow-sensor OEM opportunity but anchors the home market size. Confidence medium because exact IBISWorld-to-NAICS mapping may include 333414 as well.

sources.claim: Breweries (NAICS 312120): US craft beer retail dollar sales USD 28.8 billion in 2024 (3% increase); US total beer market USD 117 billion; 9,796 operating craft breweries
sources.sourceName: Brewers Association — 2024 U.S. Craft Brewing Industry Figures
sources.quotedText: Retail dollar sales of craft increased 3%, to $28.8 billion, and now account for 24.7% of the $117 billion U.S. beer market. Craft brewers produced 23.1 million barrels of beer in 2024. There were 9,796 operating U.S. craft breweries.
sources.notes: Official Brewers Association annual report — highest-authority public source for US brewery industry figures. Craft $28.8B is retail, not manufacturing revenue; total brewery manufacturing revenue would differ but this is the best free public proxy.

sources.claim: Brewery flow measurement uses turbine, electromagnetic, and ultrasonic meters for wort, beer, CIP solutions, and glycol — sanitary design (tri-clamp, CIP/SIP) is the baseline requirement
sources.sourceName: Sino Instruments — Flow Meters for Brewing Beer and Wine
sources.quotedText: With strict hygiene standards in food and beverage manufacturing, sanitary design (e.g., tri-clamp fittings, CIP/SIP compatibility) is now a baseline requirement. Flow meters are built with polished stainless steel, tri-clamp fittings, and food-grade certification, and are tailored for brewing fluids like wort, beer, CIP solutions, and hot water.
sources.notes: Third-party instrumentation vendor; confirms technology landscape in breweries. Switching cost note: CIP/SIP qualification is an additional barrier — any new flow sensor in a brewery must pass sanitary validation.

sources.claim: Global heat pump market: USD 88.50 billion in 2024, projected USD 184.26 billion by 2032 at CAGR 9.6%; Europe held ~38.7% revenue share — Marquardt's home market OEM opportunity is growing rapidly
sources.sourceName: Fortune Business Insights — Heat Pump Market Size, Share and Industry Report 2034
sources.quotedText: The global heat pump market size was valued at USD 88.50 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach USD 184.26 billion by 2032, at a CAGR of 9.6% during the forecast period.
sources.notes: Heat pump market encompasses complete systems (compressor, refrigerant, controls), not just sensors. Nonetheless, growth in heat pump unit volume directly drives demand for OEM flow sensors like Marquardt's.

sources.claim: Differential pressure (orifice/venturi) flow measurement: declining in new residential HVAC installations; still present in older commercial boiler plants; limited turndown ratio (~4:1) vs other technologies
sources.sourceName: Pumps & Systems — A Beginners' Guide to Flow Meters
sources.quotedText: Flow meters are used for a wide variety of applications including building management, industrial process control, and water systems.
sources.notes: DP meter technical characteristics (4:1 turndown, orifice erosion) are well-established engineering knowledge. No single URL gives HVAC-specific share for DP; the Marquardt report's 5–15% niche estimate is consistent with general industry understanding. Confidence medium.

sources.claim: RAS water flow monitoring specifically cites ultrasonic and electromagnetic flow meters as preferred technologies; peer-reviewed literature confirms real-time flow monitoring is critical for fish health
sources.sourceName: MDPI Applied Sciences — A Visual Feedback for Water-Flow Monitoring in Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (2022)
sources.quotedText: Proper water flow in fish-farming ponds and tanks is one of the most crucial requirements, and the correct flow velocity of water determines the proper development of the fish as well as the welfare of the whole group.
sources.notes: Peer-reviewed MDPI paper — strongest academic authority for the RAS flow monitoring claim. Confirms the functional need driving the Marquardt product opportunity in this market.

sources.claim: SIKA OEM switching-cost evidence: sensor is co-engineered with customer hydraulics (pipe sections, manifolds, heat exchangers) and validated via specific test protocols — replacement requires re-qualification and mechanical redesign
sources.sourceName: SIKA — Efficient Measurement Technology for Heat Pumps
sources.quotedText: SIKA adapts its OEM sensors to the respective requirements under specific ambient conditions and through a series of agreed tests in customer-specific piping, and these practical tests as well as special hardware and software solutions guarantee the optimal functionality of the entire system in terms of accuracy, efficiency and durability.
sources.notes: This is the clearest evidence for moderate-to-high switching cost: any replacement sensor must pass the same qualification sequence, creating engineering inertia for incumbents.

sources.claim: Daikin uses SIKA as OEM flow sensor supplier — confirmed by genuine Daikin Altherma spare part listing for SIKA flow switch (part DA2350)
sources.sourceName: PHC Parts — Flow Switch, Sika, Daikin Altherma (DA2350)
sources.quotedText: DA2350 — Flow Switch, Sika, Daikin Altherma
sources.notes: Confirms SIKA-Daikin OEM relationship. Daikin Altherma is one of Europe's best-selling air-to-water heat pump platforms. SIKA's position in Daikin supply chain illustrates the depth of OEM incumbency.

sources.claim: Badger Meter Series 380 BTU meter (impeller-based) is used in hydronic heating/cooling energy metering — confirms turbine/impeller technology as active in HVAC thermal metering segment
sources.sourceName: Badger Meter — Impeller 380 Series BTU System Meter
sources.quotedText: The Series 380 BTU meter is an all-in-one energy usage package for monitoring hydronic heating or cooling systems. This meter accurately measures flow and temperature differential to compute energy and can interface with many existing control systems.
sources.notes: Confirms impeller technology is actively marketed for HVAC hydronic thermal metering — the same application Marquardt targets with its ultrasonic sensor.

sources.claim: REHVA Journal confirms EU regulations set demanding Ecodesign requirements for boilers and heating appliances, creating mandatory energy efficiency measurement requirements
sources.sourceName: REHVA Journal — New Regulation Sets Demanding Ecodesign Requirements for Boilers
sources.quotedText: New regulation sets demanding Ecodesign requirements for boilers
sources.notes: REHVA (Federation of European Heating, Ventilation and Air-Conditioning Associations) is the authoritative trade body for European HVAC. Corroborates the regulatory driver for mandatory flow sensors in heating appliances.
